title: selinum chabraei
intro: Selinum chabraei, also known as Chabra's fern or Chabre's fern, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the Himalayan region and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Selinum chabraei can be prepared into herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions due to its potent medicinal properties.
